incorrects.description: Student selected A) 4 × 6. Same product (24) but different structure than 3 × 8.

steps.text: You picked 4 rows of 6. That makes 24 dots, but it's not the same array turned. Look at the reference: 3 rows of 8. If you rotate that, how many rows would you get?

incorrects.description: Student selected C) 6 × 4. Same product (24) but different structure than 3 × 8.

steps.text: You picked 6 rows of 4. That makes 24 dots, but it's not a rotation of 3 rows of 8. When you rotate an array, the rows become columns and the columns become rows.

incorrects.description: Student selected D) 2 × 12. Same product (24) but different structure than 3 × 8.

steps.text: You picked 2 rows of 12. That makes 24 dots, but it's not the same array rotated. The reference has 3 rows of 8. When you rotate, those 3 rows become 3 columns.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Reference array rotates 90 degrees. 3 rows of 8 become 8 rows of 3.

steps.text: Here, let me show you. Watch the reference array rotate. The 3 rows become 3 columns. The 8 in each row becomes 8 rows. So 3 rows of 8 turns into 8 rows of 3. The answer is B, 8 × 3. That's the same array rotated.
